title: Cache invalidation change without stale-data coverage should block
diff:
diff --git a/src/orders/useCancelOrder.ts b/src/orders/useCancelOrder.ts
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/src/orders/useCancelOrder.ts
+++ b/src/orders/useCancelOrder.ts
@@ -1 +1 @@
-queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['orders'] })
+queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['order', orderId] })

calibration.whyThisMatters: Cache invalidation key changed and stale list behavior is unverified.

acceptableAlternatives.when: The expected cache relationship between order detail and order list is not known.

evidence.summary: Cache invalidation key changed without stale-data coverage.

requiredCoverage: order list is refreshed or remains correct after cancellation
order detail cache remains correct after cancellation
missingCoverage: stale order list behavior after cancellation
blockers: High-risk cache invalidation change lacks meaningful stale-data coverage.
recommendedTests: Add an integration test proving canceled order state is reflected in the affected views.
residualRisks: Users may see stale order data after cancellation.
expectedDecision.summary: Cache invalidation behavior changed and needs stale-data coverage.
